title: Fuse sensors using time and uncertainty
description: Combine measurements with a state estimator that accounts for each sensor's timestamp, rate, and noise, and degrades safely when one drops.
body: Use when multiple sensors estimate the same state (pose, velocity). Do not average raw readings ignoring their noise and timing, and avoid trusting a single sensor without a fallback. Actions: use a Kalman/EKF-style estimator that predicts on the fast input and corrects on each measurement using its covariance and timestamp, and define timeout/outlier handling so a dropped or stale sensor widens uncertainty instead of corrupting the estimate. Validation signals include a fusion test where a dropped measurement degrades gracefully and the estimate tracks ground truth within its covariance. Risk boundary: a fusion that diverges on a dropped sensor can feed a bad state to control and cause unsafe motion; treat missing timeout/outlier handling as a high-severity gap.
